name: torchinfo (PyTorch Model Summary)

performance.notes: Single forward pass; minimal overhead; handles nested modules correctly

agent_readiness.notes: Replacement for torchsummary — handles complex models with named parameters. from torchinfo import summary. summary(model, input_size=(1, 3, 224, 224)). Output: layer name, output shape, param count, trainable params. summary(model, input_data=x) for variable inputs. col_names=['input_size', 'output_size', 'num_params', 'mult_adds']. depth parameter for nested module expansion. Batch dimension handling. Works with Transformers (attention masks, multiple inputs). Estimates FLOPs (mult_adds). Successor to torchsummary with better multi-input support.

gotchas: Requires actual input size or sample input — can't summarize without forward pass
FLOP estimates are approximate (mult-adds only, not all ops)
Very large models may print very long output
best_when: Quick PyTorch model architecture overview before training
Verifying output shapes layer-by-layer for debugging
avoid_when: Detailed FLOPs analysis (use fvcore or thop instead)
summary: torchinfo prints beautiful PyTorch model summaries — replacement for torchsummary. summary(model, input_size=(1, 3, 224, 224)) shows each layer's output shape, param count, trainable status. Works with Transformers (multiple inputs via input_data=). depth= for nested module expansion. Estimates mult-adds (FLOPs). Essential one-liner for model inspection. Better than torchsummary for modern PyTorch architectures.
